what_it_does: Defines a DTCG-style design token vocabulary for colors, chrome materials, spacing, radius, and typography, with dna metadata for color provenance, OKLCH values, derivation rules, and measured references.
observations: Top-level description frames the file as a Design DNA token vocabulary where sampled colors are source-derived and formula rules drive space, radius, and type.
Color tokens are grouped into surface, accent, ink, semantic, and an ordered axis_ramp gradient.
Most color tokens carry hex values, plain descriptions, OKLCH metadata, and pixel-sampled provenance.
axis_ramp stores an ordered warm-metal scale and a rule for ordinal coloring with OKLCH interpolation.
chrome defines glass and frost material objects with background, alpha, blur_px, and ink values.
space, radius, and display type tokens include derivation formulas plus measured values where available.
type defines display sizes, reading text sizes, and two font family tokens.
standouts: The file preserves both token values and the reasoning/provenance behind many values in $extensions.dna.
Several descriptions encode usage constraints such as accent gold never being a fill and semantic colors being small marks only.
The axis_ramp is explicitly ordered and tied to an ordinal coloring rule.
conventions: Uses snake_case token names for multiword entries.
Uses reference aliases such as {space.snug} and {space.standard}.
Separates sampled color data from formula-derived spacing, radius, and type data.
concerns: Some formula-derived values differ from measured values, such as space.snug 32px versus measured 34px and type.title_lg 41px versus measured 40px.
chrome is marked as estimated and needing confirmation against product.
axis_ramp lacks the same provenance field used by individual sampled color tokens.
notes: A design-token ledger that records values alongside local rationale, provenance, derivation, and usage constraints.
questions: Should measured values be preserved only as evidence, or should they affect emitted token values?
Should chrome tokens remain in this file while they are explicitly marked as estimated?
Should axis_ramp include provenance metadata like the individual color tokens?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it is a DTCG-style token vocabulary with surface and accent sampling plus derivation metadata for spacing, radius, and type.
apparent_themes: usage constraints embedded in token descriptions
intention_signals: It seems intended to make design decisions reproducible by storing not only token values but also the source sampling, derivation rules, and usage boundaries that explain how the tokens should behave.
novelty: The token file treats design tokens as a small evidence ledger by pairing ordinary DTCG values with dna metadata, measured references, and behavioral rules.
contribution: Contributes a structured design-token substrate covering color, material chrome, spacing, radius, and typography, including provenance and derivation metadata that a fused system would keep as interpretive context for visual generation and implementation.
summary_for_embedding: DTCG-style design token JSON for counterpart-design defining sampled colors, chrome materials, spacing, radius, typography, OKLCH metadata, derivation formulas, measured references, and usage constraints.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for preserving a design DNA vocabulary so implementers can use token values together with the provenance, formulas, and constraints behind them.
